statement.p: A farmer purchased 340 meters of fencing, and will build a rectangular pen along a river. This implies the pen has only 3 fenced sides. To enclose the largest possible area, what should the pens length and width be? Model the pens area with a function, and then find its maximum value.
To enclose the largest possible area, the pens length and width should be meters.

solution.p: Assume the rectangles length is l meters, and its width is w meters. Since the pen has only three fenced sides, we have:
The area of the pen is A=lw\text{.} Substitute l=340-2w into A=lw\text{,} we have:
Next, we use technology to graph the function A=-2w^2+340w\text{,} and then find the parabolas vertex (85,{14450})\text{.}
According to the vertex point (85,{14450})\text{,} when the pens width is 85 meters, the pen has the maximum area of {14450} square meters.
To maximize the pens area, the length and width should be {170} meters and 85 meters.
